pattern: With only two letters, you never need more than two moves: one to remove all 'a's and one for all 'b's — unless the whole string is already a palindrome (one move) or empty (zero moves).
hints: Any string over the alphabet {'a','b'} can be emptied in at most 2 steps — in the worst case remove all 'a's as one palindromic subsequence, then all 'b's as another.
If the string itself is already a palindrome you can remove it entirely in a single step.
The answer is therefore 0 (empty), 1 (already a palindrome), or 2 (otherwise). You only need to check for emptiness and then check whether the string reads the same forwards and backwards.

edge_inputs.description: already a palindrome with both letters
not a palindrome — needs two steps
long all-'a' string — one palindrome removal
alternating letters — not a palindrome, two steps

stress.description: random strings of 'a' and 'b' up to length 1000
